scenario_results.reason: The specialist produced an evidence-backed workforce assignment handoff with prior owner, next owner, required artifacts, blocker state, and closure criteria without overstating authority.
The specialist blocked the workforce handoff until job order, worker-status, skills, safety, timekeeping, and system links were supplied.
Ambiguous ownership across staffing, client service delivery, HR, and safety triggered dependency escalation rather than dropped or duplicated assignment work.
Conflicting WFM, client roster, and service desk records stayed explicit and were escalated for source-hierarchy resolution.
The lane refused overtime, worker classification, training waiver, and client staffing commitment approval and routed to adjacent owners.
Temporary-worker safety readiness was treated as an industry-specific blocker requiring host-worksite and staffing authority evidence.
Recruiting, onboarding, HR-record changes, payroll corrections, and contract updates were rejected as execution work and routed outward.
The incomplete handoff was paused with missing prior owner, job order, assignment roster, evidence bundle, due date, blocker state, and destination-owner acknowledgement captured.
The lane enforced NAICS 561 support-service and employment-service boundaries and routed hiring, payroll, pricing, and safety signoff outside the lane.
Missing tenant retrieval hooks and authoritative systems triggered low-confidence escalation instead of confident routing or closure.
Conflicting SOW, tenant safety policy, and queue playbook requirements were escalated without unsupported synthesis.
Closure confirmation required destination-owner acknowledgement, blocker resolution, and workforce artifact completeness.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass at the 0.92 threshold for validated validation.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all coordination-handoffs-and-routing abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability required by the issue contract.
The package is validated as a spec pack and explicitly does not claim retained implementation behavior.
